title: Carex horizontalis
taxonomy: Carex horizontalis is classified as a type of sedge, which is a member of the family Cyperaceae. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and morphology, including its flat, spreading habit and distinctive leaf and flower structures. As a member of the Carex genus, Carex horizontalis is further classified as a species within the section and section Carex. Within the section, Carex horizontalis is often distinguished by its unique growth habit and other morphological characteristics. The classification of Carex horizontalis provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the genus.
uses: Carex horizontalis is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. Its leaves and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat respiratory problems and skin infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Carex horizontalis.

constituents: Carex horizontalis is a species of sedge that is native to North America. It is a low-growing, spreading groundcover that forms a dense mat of leaves.
